LAW AND MOTION 302, RECEIVER SUSAN L. UECKER'S MOTION FOR ORDER APPROVING RECEIVER'S FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT, DISCHARGE OF RECEIVER, EXONERATION OF RECEIVER'S BOND AND APPROVAL OF COMPENSATION AND EXPENSES OF RECEIVER. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. ELINA TACHKOVA-DAVENPORT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CHALLENGING THE RECEIVER'S REPORT. -THE $33,000 DRAW IS A 2005 TRANSACTION. -THE REQUEST FOR A $45,000 CREDIT IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT THE PAYMENTS WERE FOR. -THE REQUEST RELATED TO 4 CHECKS IN EX. C. IS DENIED. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT THE CHECKS WERE FOR AND THE CHECKS HAVE NOT BEEN AUTHENTICATED. -TACHKOVA-DAVENPORT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT SHE IS ENTITLED TO A CREDIT OF $1,468.77 OR $37.19 FOR A MONEY ORDER OR THAT THE RECEIVER FAILED TO REFERENCE ALL PURCHASES AND SALES. -THE PARTIES ARE TO SPLIT THE COST OF 2008 TAX RETURNS. IN ADDITION, THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST TO DISSOLVE THE LLC. RECEIVER TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Approving Receiver'S Final Account And Report, Discharge Of Receiver, Exoneration Of Receiver'S Bond And Approval Of Compensation And Expenses Of Receiver; Receiver'S Final Report And Account And Exonaration Of Receiver'S Bond And Declaratin Of Susan L. Uecker.
Off Calendar Dec-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RECEIVER SUSAN L. UECKER'S Motion For Order Approving Receiver's Final Account And Report, Discharge Of Receiver, Exoneration Of Receiver's Bond And Approval Of Compensation And Expenses Of Receiver.
APPEARANCES: Walter W. Gouldsbury, III, Esq. of Jeffer, Mangels, Butler & Marmaro LLP 398-8080 for receiver & moving party; Elina Tachkova-Davenport, 377-2121 deft/x-complainant & opposing party in propria persona; Oleg Tachkov, 925/899-1322 in propria persona.
RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. ELINA TACHKOVA-DAVENPORT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CHALLENGING THE RECEIVER'S REPORT.
-THE $33,000 DRAW IS A 2005 TRANSACTION.
-THE REQUEST FOR A $45,000 CREDIT IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT THE PAYMENTS WERE FOR.
-THE REQUEST RELATED TO 4 CHECKS IN EX. C. IS DENIED. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT THE CHECKS WERE FOR AND THE CHECKS HAVE NOT BEEN AUTHENTICATED.
-TACHKOVA-DAVENPORT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT SHE IS ENTITLED TO A CREDIT OF $1,468.77 OR $37.19 FOR A MONEY ORDER OR THAT THE RECEIVER FAILED TO REFERENCE ALL PURCHASES AND SALES.
-THE PARTIES ARE TO SPLIT THE COST OF 2008 TAX RETURNS.
In addition, the Court granted the request to dissolve the LLC. Receiver to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, And/Or Evidence Sanctions, Or, Alternatively, For An Order Compelling Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jaime C. Uziel for defendant/Cross-complainant and moving party: Elina Tachova-Davenport

NO APPEARANCE Stated. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Elina Tachkova-Davenport's 's motion for terminating, issue, and / or evidentiary sanctions or for an order compelling responses to request for production of documents is denied in part and granted in part.
The motion for terminating, issue, and / or evidentiary sanctions is denied. The motion to compel further responses to the request for production of documents and to produce documents is granted. Plaintiff has not filed a responsive separate statement of matters in dispute. The preliminary statement and objections, other than based on the attorney client and attorney work product privileges, are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to serve supplemental responses and produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. If any documents are withheld based on a claim of privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. The privilege log, if any, shall be served at the same time as the supplemental responses and documents. The Court finds that plaintiff asserted unmeritorious objections. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $790 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
